title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The Silk Road was an ancient network of trade routes (0) that connected the East and West for over a thousand years. (9) Although it was named after the lucrative Chinese silk trade, many other goods and ideas traveled along these paths. (10) Such was the importance of this network that it facilitated the spread of religions, technologies, and even diseases across continents. (11) Provided that merchants could survive the treacherous terrain, the potential profits from their journey were enormous. (12) Not only did the Silk Road boost local economies, but it also fostered cultural exchange between diverse civilizations. (13) Regardless of the political changes in the regions it crossed, the demand for exotic products kept the routes active. (14) Not until the rise of maritime trade did the land-based Silk Road begin to lose its primary significance. (15) Despite its eventual decline, its legacy remains a testament to the enduring human desire for connection and discovery. (16) Whether or not we can see its influence today in modern global trade is a question for historians to explore. This historic route reminds us that globalization is not a new concept, but a process that has shaped our world for many centuries.

example.text: The Silk Road was an ancient network of trade routes (0) that connected the East and West.
example.explanation: Relative pronoun referring to the 'network of trade routes'.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast.
Determiner used for emphasis at the start of a clause.
Conjunction meaning 'if' or 'on the condition that'.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' correlative conjunction.
Part of the fixed linker 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('did the... Silk Road begin').
Preposition used to show contrast or concession.
